title: Living in Boulder vlog 4 - 25 - 2017: BouldeRoman Timeline

enrichment.summary: Kyle vlogs from Boulder about sobering up due to running out of money, and explains a Roman-calendar-based genealogical timeline he's building for his website and TV show concept. He describes a generational cycle system (every 33 years) inspired by Herodotus and a book about generational patterns, applied to a single fictional family spanning thousands of years.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Being sober makes complex creative work like the timeline easier to manage
A big unfinished website makes the creator look crazy; polishing is the priority for the rest of the year
33-year generations and their cyclical patterns are a legitimate and fascinating framework for understanding history
Reluctant to go further into debt; trying to avoid credit card and borrowing from mom again

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle is currently living in Boulder, Vila.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle is more or less sober now because he ran out of money and can't buy more.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle doesn't want to go into credit card debt again or borrow more money.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle borrowed $12.50 from his mom.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle feels cornered but believes he can do a better job with his timeline if he's sober.

claims.subject: Kyle's timeline
claims.claim: Kyle's timeline was never finished and was only halfway through.

claims.subject: Kyle's timeline
claims.claim: Kyle was still in the designing stage when his car broke down, which led to abandoning the timeline.

claims.subject: Kyle's timeline
claims.claim: Kyle worked on the timeline again in the latter half of 2012 and the beginning of 2013.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle plans to work full force on his website until the end of April.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle will focus on sober, well-rounded life maintenance tasks next week.

claims.subject: Kyle's car
claims.claim: Kyle's mom is helping him sell his car.

claims.subject: Kyle's car
claims.claim: Kyle's mom offered to drive the car to her house but didn't let him lower the price.

claims.subject: Kyle's car
claims.claim: Kyle's car is worth $23, and everything is new on the engine.

claims.subject: Kyle's website
claims.claim: Kyle added a new feature to his website that he had been thinking about since 2011.

claims.subject: Kyle's timeline
claims.claim: Kyle's timeline starts with the Roman calendar.

claims.subject: Kyle's timeline
claims.claim: Kyle's timeline aligns with the Roman calendar, so 244 in the Roman calendar is also 244 in his calendar.

claims.subject: Kyle's timeline
claims.claim: Kyle created generations in his timeline every 33 years.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle listened to the Young Turks discussing generational cycles and believes in the concept.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle identifies as part of Generation X, which he considers the lazy generation.

claims.subject: Kyle's timeline
claims.claim: Kyle's timeline includes patterns every 14 generations, cycling back to the same characteristics.

claims.subject: Kyle's timeline
claims.claim: Kyle's timeline features one family for his television show, with each character living 100 years.

claims.subject: Kyle's timeline
claims.claim: In Kyle's timeline, each character has a kid at 33, knows their grandkid, and dies at 100.

claims.subject: Kyle's website
claims.claim: Kyle acknowledges that his website is huge and needs polishing to avoid confusing people.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle plans to spend the rest of the year polishing and finishing his website.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle prefers not to use a green screen for his YouTube videos because it looks unprofessional.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle forgot to turn the lights on for his video and hopes to fast-forward to the part where the lights are on.
